headline: Delta Tel Aviv Restart Tests New Leadership And International Growth Story
summary: Delta Air Lines has resumed commercial flights between New York JFK and Tel Aviv. The route restart follows a suspension during heightened conflict in the Middle East. Delta is among the first major US carriers to restore this long haul service. For investors watching NYSE:DAL, the decision to restart JFK to Tel Aviv service puts fresh attention on Delta's international footprint and operational choices. The stock last closed at $58.78, with a 1 year return of 27.3% and a 3 year return of...

headline: Iran-Israel Conflict Driven Fuel Cost Spike Hits United Airlines (UAL) Results
summary: United Airlines Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:UAL) is one of the 10 best large cap growth stocks to buy. After the airline issued a warning that surging fuel prices could affect its short-term financial performance, shares of United Airlines Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:UAL) declined. headline: Will Multi‑State eVTOL Pilots and FAA Test Milestones Change Joby Aviation's (JOBY) Narrative?
summary: Earlier this month, Joby Aviation announced it had completed piloted electric air taxi demonstration flights across the San Francisco Bay Area and begun flight testing its first FAA-conforming aircraft, while also being chosen as a partner in the White House-backed eVTOL Integration Pilot Program to start early operations across 10 U.S. states. These developments highlight how Joby is pairing regulatory progress with real-world urban flight demonstrations and an integrated ecosystem with...

headline: How TSA slowdowns may add to travel demand woes
summary: As geopolitical tensions and oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F) put US airlines in a bind, the partial government shutdown has reportedly led to massive slowdowns at major airports' TSA checkpoints (Transportation Security Administration). Steve Trent, an independent aviation specialist, comments on what these slowdowns may mean for travel demand trends. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

content.title: How TSA slowdowns may add to travel demand woes
content.description:
As geopolitical tensions and oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F) put US airlines in a bind, the partial government shutdown has reportedly led to massive slowdowns at major airports' TSA checkpoints (Transportation Security Administration).

Steve Trent, an independent aviation specialist, comments on what these slowdowns may mean for travel demand trends.

To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.
